posture: Inform — a clean scan: no fresh, dated NSW-REZ development cleared the relevance bar in the 24 hours to Friday morning. The free source register produced no new agency release, market notice, planning step or developer disclosure with a REZ nexus: EnergyCo's latest-updates feed showed nothing published inside the window, the AEMO market-notices feed carried no new REZ-region constraint or connection event, and the New England network-operator, corridor-refinement and Central-West Orana pages held no new dated material. This week's two fresh signals both landed earlier and are now deduplicated out as prior-digest carry-forwards: the NSW Farmers Association annual conference (Rosehill Gardens, wrapped 22 July) formally carrying its energy-transition motions — the resolution urging the NSW Government to cut the New England REZ from 8GW to 3GW, the motion against compulsory acquisition of land for transmission, the motion obliging EnergyCo to reassess all REZ transmission requirements, plus motions against renewables subsidies and for independent baseline soil/water testing (carried in the 23 July digest); and Energy Minister Penny Sharpe's $14.5m Central-West Orana Telecommunications Infrastructure Uplift Program (also 23 July). Two ACM regional stories that surfaced in the scan resolved to late-June material on inspection and were dropped as stale: the Northern Daily Leader's account of the government skipping the 25 June Walcha transmission-line parliamentary debate (published 26 June) and the Armidale Express line on Kellie Sloane's REZ-cap position (a restatement of the Coalition's late-June pledge already carried). Every other live thread is an unchanged carry-forward and is deduplicated out: Squadron Energy's 16 July decision not to proceed with the 426MW Boorolong wind project inside the New England REZ and the political fight it fed; the New England Draft REZ Access Scheme Declaration on exhibition (feedback by 6 August, declaration targeted end-September), with the EIS expected to be lodged and exhibited in the second half of 2026 and energisation targeted for 2032; the refined 1km Muswellbrook-Walcha corridor and its July-September field-investigation program across roughly 150 affected landholders, against live Walcha High Country Guardians opposition and the 22,000-signature petition; EnergyCo's 15 July South West REZ Project Development Deed with Transgrid (Jerilderie-Wagga Wagga, unlocking 3.56GW); the Illawarra REZ Community Reference Group membership call (20 July); and on Central-West Orana the Port-to-REZ OSOM delivery campaign with turbine-blade deliveries to Uungula beginning this month, the Main Road No. 709 gazettal, ACEREZ's staged access-track works under stronger heritage controls with the DPHI rock-shelter assessment still open, and the community roadshow flagged for 11-15 August. Source gaps to log rather than read as absence of news: ABC News and The Guardian Australia surfaced no new dated REZ item in this run, and the paid mastheads (AFR, SMH, the ACM regional titles) produced no fresh REZ headline even at standfirst level, so no paywalled items are carried today. Nothing in the scan changes the standing posture: the dominant exposures remain the New England political and social-licence contest ahead of the March 2027 state election, and the delivery and heritage risks on Central-West Orana.
summary: No fresh NSW-REZ-relevant item surfaced in the 24-hour window to Friday 24 July morning — a clean scan across the free source register, with no new EnergyCo release, AEMO market notice, planning step or developer disclosure carrying a REZ nexus. This week's fresh signals (the NSW Farmers conference carrying its motion to cut the New England REZ to 3GW and oppose compulsory acquisition, and Minister Sharpe's $14.5m Central-West Orana telecommunications uplift) both landed on 23 July and are prior-digest carry-forwards. Two ACM regional stories in the scan resolved to late-June material (the 26 June account of the skipped Walcha parliamentary debate; the Sloane REZ-cap restatement) and were dropped as stale. All other active threads — the Squadron Boorolong exit, the New England Access Scheme exhibition (feedback by 6 August), the July-September corridor field investigations and Walcha opposition, the 15 July South West REZ Transgrid deed, the Illawarra Community Reference Group call, and the Central-West Orana OSOM campaign, Main Road No. 709 gazettal and ACEREZ heritage matter — are unchanged carry-forwards. ABC News, The Guardian Australia and the paid mastheads (AFR, SMH, ACM titles) surfaced no new dated REZ item and are logged as a source gap. The digest is published with empty sections so the archive shows continuity.
